questions.question: What is the main constraint that prevents storing media files directly on-chain like text messages?
options: Blockchain encryption doesn't work for large files
Storage costs make it economically impractical ($4000 for a 50MB video on mainnet)
Smart contracts can't handle binary data
IPFS is required by the EVM for media files
questions.correctAnswer: Storage costs make it economically impractical ($4000 for a 50MB video on mainnet)

questions.question: In the hybrid storage architecture for time-locked media, where does the encrypted media content actually live?
The blocklock encryption mechanism changes significantly when adapting from messages to media files.
What does the smart contract store for time-locked media instead of the actual encrypted bytes?
options: A reference (IPFS hash) to the encrypted content
A compressed version of the media
questions.correctAnswer: A reference (IPFS hash) to the encrypted content

questions.question: In the MediaRelease struct, the ______ field stores the location identifier for the encrypted content on IPFS.
Why does the contract inherit from AbstractBlocklockReceiver?
options: To get access to decryption request functions and callback routing from dcipher
questions.correctAnswer: To get access to decryption request functions and callback routing from dcipher

questions.question: The contract decrypts the media file on-chain in the _onBlocklockReceived callback, just like it does for text messages.
